title: Instrument d'adhésion
text_preview: 19280002 68 LYNDON B. JOHNSON President of the United States of America IO ALL IO WHOM THESE PRESENTS SHALL COME, GREETING: KNOW YE That, whereas a convention relating to international exhibitions was signed at Paris on November 22, 1928, and two protocols modifying the convention were signed at Paris on May 10, 1948 and November 16, 1966, all in the French language; AND WHEREAS the Government of France, as deposita…

pages.text:
NOW, THEREFORE, be

it known that I, Lyndon B. Johnson,

President of the United States of America, having

seen and

considered the aforesaid convention and protocols, do hereby, in pursuance of the aforesaid advice and consent of the Senate of the United States of America, approve accession of the United

States of America thereto.

IN TESTIMONY WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the Seal of the United States of America to be affixed.

DONE at the city of Washington this sixth day of

May in the year of

and and of the Independence

Of the United States of

America the one hundred ninety-second.

By the President:

HeauRusk

Secretary of State
